review_rounds.summary: policy_sync is fully surfaced on the CLI's real UX surfaces (execute status/next text and --json, and the gate-binding JSON consumed by verify record / review record / import-ci) across every state (synced/unchanged/skipped/failed), and non-policy sections stay frozen exactly as specced. Verified by driving the actual bin/vibepro.js binary end-to-end, not just reading code: execute start -> status -> next -> verify record --json against a throwaway git fixture, mutating the parent config to exercise all four policy_sync outcomes on both text and --json output. Single-refresh consolidation leaves branch_match/dirty/head fields unaffected.

findings.detail: renderVerificationEvidenceSummary()（verify recordの非--json text出力）はwarningsのみ表示しmanaged_worktree_contextを出さないため、verify record実行中のgate/context refreshで起きたpolicy_sync失敗はそのコマンドのtext出力に現れない（execute status/nextでは完全に可視、--jsonでも可視）。pre-existingパターン（dirty等も同様に非表示）でregressionではなくnon-blocking。follow-up起票済み。

review_rounds.judgment_delta: initial concern: plumbing change could regress freshness of unrelated fields or leave failure visible only in --json -> pass because verified against the real CLI binary that single-refresh keeps other fields intact, default text output flags failed sync in headline and detail, all four states + frozen sections behave as specced, and the gate-binding JSON carries policy_sync with last_event
